Unseen Depths: Subtext and Symbolism in Jaws

X by: Finn N. Waters — March 15, 2024

In the balmy summer of 1975, Jaws famously sent shockwaves across the globe, forever altering our collective relationship with the ocean. Yet, beneath the film’s skin-prickling suspense and iconic one-liners lies a depth often unexplored—the weaving of subtext and symbolism that underpins this landmark cinematic treasure.

Swimming Against the Current: Jaws and the rise of shark advocacy

X by: Bruce — March 14, 2024

Decades have passed since Jaws first frightened swimmers away from beachfronts, gripping the world with a fear as unyielding as the ocean’s tide. The visceral reaction to Steven Spielberg’s 1975 iconic shark thriller became more than just another cinematic experience; it sparked a worldwide surge in shark hysteria. However, amidst the ripples of terror, an unanticipated undercurrent arose: shark advocacy.
